# Stochastic Block Model and Multitype Branching Process

## SBM definition
$Q=[q]$ types/colors, type fractions $\alpha=(\alpha_k)$, symmetric $B=(B_{k\ell})\in\mathbb R_+^{q\times q}$.

**$\text{SBM}_n(B,\alpha)$.** Each $i\in[n]$ independently draws color $k(i)\sim\alpha$. Conditional on colors, edges are independent with $\Pr(ij\in E) = B_{k(i),k(j)}/n$.

⇒ A vertex of color $k$ has $\text{Bin}(n_\ell, B_{k\ell}/n) \approx \text{Poi}(B_{k\ell}\alpha_\ell) =: \text{Poi}(C_{k\ell})$ neighbors of color $\ell$. Mean matrix $C = (C_{k\ell})$.

---

## Multitype Branching Process (MTBP)

Type-$i$ parent has $\vec X_i = (X_{i1},\dots,X_{iq})$ children, with $X_{ij}$ = #type-$j$ children. Joint PGF
$$G_i(\vec\lambda) = \sum_{\vec k}\Pr(\vec X_i=\vec k)\prod_j\lambda_j^{k_j}.$$
Mean matrix $C_{ij}=\mathbb E[X_{ij}]$.

**SBM example.** $X_{ij}\sim\text{Poi}(C_{ij})$ independent ⇒ $G_i(\vec\lambda) = \exp\Big(\sum_j C_{ij}(\lambda_j-1)\Big)$.

### Generation-$t$ mean
Let $Z_{ij}(t)$ = #type-$j$ descendants in generation $t$ from a type-$i$ root.
$$\mathbb E[Z_{ij}(t+1)\mid \vec Z_i(t)] = \sum_\ell \mathbb E[X_{\ell j}]Z_{i\ell}(t) \;\Rightarrow\; \mathbb E[Z_{ij}(t)] = (C^t)_{ij}.$$

---

## Reducibility / irreducibility
$B$ is **reducible** iff $\exists k\neq\ell$ with $(B^t)_{k\ell}=0$ $\forall t$ (no path between types). Equivalent: types cluster into groups with no inter-group paths. **Irreducible** = otherwise.

## Lemma (Subcritical: extinction)
If $\lambda_{\max}(C) < 1$, then $\eta_i = \Pr(\text{extinction}\mid\text{root type }i) = 1$ ($\theta_i = 0$).

**Proof.** $\Pr(\vec Z_i(t)\neq 0) \leq \mathbb E[\sum_j Z_{ij}(t)] = \sum_j(C^t)_{ij} \leq O(t^{q-1}\lambda_{\max}^t) \to 0$. (For symmetric $C$, drop the $t^{q-1}$ factor.) So $\theta_i = \lim_{t\to\infty}\Pr(\vec Z_i(t)\neq 0) = 0$.

## Lemma 1 (Iterated PGF gives extinction-by-$t$)
$\eta_i(t) = \Pr(\vec Z_i(t)=0)$ satisfies $\eta_i(t+1) = G_i(\vec\eta(t))$, $\vec\eta(0)=\vec 0$.

**Proof (induction).** $t=0$: $\eta_i(1) = \Pr(\vec X_i=\vec 0) = G_i(\vec 0)$. Step: condition on first generation $\vec X_i = \vec k$. Each of the $\sum k_j$ children initiates an indep copy of the BP; all must die in $t$ generations:
$$\Pr(\vec Z_i(t+1)=0\mid \vec X_i=\vec k) = \prod_j \eta_j(t)^{k_j}.$$
Sum over $\vec k$ weighted by $P_i(\vec k)$ gives $G_i(\vec\eta(t))$.

## Lemma 2 (Monotonicity)
$\vec\eta(t)\uparrow\vec\eta$ where $\eta_i = \Pr(\text{extinction})$.

(Events $\{Z_i(t)=0\}$ nested ⇒ probabilities monotone; bounded by 1.)

## Theorem (Fixed-point characterization)
(a) $\eta_i(t)\uparrow\eta_i$.
(b) $\vec\eta = \vec G(\vec\eta)$.
(c) $\vec\eta$ is the **smallest** fixed point in $[0,1]^q$: if $\vec G(\vec\lambda)=\vec\lambda$ then $\vec\eta\leq\vec\lambda$.

**Proof.**
(a) Lemmas 1+2.
(b) $G_i$ monotone+continuous in coordinates ⇒ $\eta_i(t+1)=G_i(\vec\eta(t))\uparrow G_i(\vec\eta)$, but also $\eta_i(t+1)\uparrow\eta_i$ ⇒ $\eta_i = G_i(\vec\eta)$.
(c) $\vec G(\vec\lambda)=\vec\lambda$, $\vec 0\leq\vec\lambda$: $\eta_i(1)=G_i(\vec 0)\leq G_i(\vec\lambda)=\lambda_i$. Iterate: $\eta_i(t)\leq\lambda_i$ ⇒ $\eta_i\leq\lambda_i$.

## Theorem (Survival ⇔ supercritical)
For irreducible, non-singular MTBP: $\theta_i = 1-\eta_i > 0$ for all $i$ ⇔ $\lambda_{\max}(C) > 1$.

(Singular = each parent has exactly one child a.s., trivially never extinct.)

---

## Application to SBM (giant component)

## Theorem (Giant component on SBM)
Assume $C$ irreducible.
(a) If $\lambda_{\max}(C) < 1$: w.h.p. $|C_{\max}| = O(\log n)$.
(b) If $\lambda_{\max}(C) > 1$: $\exists\theta>0$ with $|C_{\max}|/n \to \theta$ in probability, where
$$\theta = \sum_i \alpha_i\theta_i,\qquad \theta_i = 1 - G_i(\vec\eta) = 1 - e^{-\sum_j C_{ij}\theta_j}\;\text{(SBM)}.$$
Off-giant components: $\max_{x\notin C_{\max}}|C(x)| = O(\log n)$.

**Proof sketch.**
1. **BFS coupling.** Local exploration of SBM at a type-$i$ vertex couples to MTBP$(C)$ for $O(\log n)$ generations.
2. **Subcritical.** All BFS trees stay tiny ⇒ tail bound on $|C(x)|$ via mean of $\sum_j(C^t)_{ij} = O(t^{q-1}\lambda_{\max}^t)$.
3. **Supercritical.** Define $V_i^+ = \{x: k(x)=i, |C(x)|>K\log n\}$. BFS gives $|V_i^+|/n \to \theta_i\alpha_i$ in probability. A sprinkling argument (small-tree lemma + connectivity of "big" pieces above a threshold) shows all of $\bigcup_i V_i^+$ merges into a single giant: $||C_{\max}|/n - \sum_i\alpha_i\theta_i| \to 0$.

---

## Pitfalls
- Use $\lambda_{\max}(C)$, not the entries of $C$, as the criticality test.
- Smallest fixed point of $\vec G(\vec\eta)=\vec\eta$ is the extinction prob; survival is $1$ minus that.
- For SBM with type fractions: $\theta = \sum_i \alpha_i\theta_i$ (weight by type fraction).
